categories.name: Mechanics
categories.description: Classical mechanics, motion, forces, and collisions

categories.name: Waves & Optics
categories.description: Wave propagation, resonance, standing waves, and optics

categories.name: Electricity & Magnetism
categories.description: Electrostatics, electric forces, fields, and charge interactions

labs.title: Standing Waves
labs.description: Study standing waves on a cord produced by the interference of two opposite traveling waves. Explore resonant vibration modes, fundamental frequencies, and the locations of nodes and antinodes.
objectives: Measure wave propagation velocity on a cord
Identify and compare positions of nodes and antinodes
Analyze effects of tension and damping on standing waves

metadata.institution: SMU, MedTech, Mediterranean Institute of Technology

questions.title: Measure Wave Propagation Velocity
questions.description: Measure the velocity of wave propagation on the cord.
questions.instruction: Report your measured value and show the calculation steps used.

questions.title: Determine Fundamental Resonant Frequency
questions.description: Determine the fundamental resonant frequency related to cord vibration.
questions.instruction: Provide the formula used and the final computed frequency.

questions.title: Fundamental Mode Screenshot
questions.description: Set frequency to the fundamental mode, restart, wait ~15s, and capture the vibration.
questions.instruction: Attach/submit your screenshot reference and briefly describe the observed pattern.

questions.title: Nodes and Antinodes (Fundamental)
questions.description: Measure positions of nodes and antinodes for the fundamental mode.
questions.instruction: List measured positions and include units.

questions.title: Second Harmonic Frequency
questions.description: Calculate the frequency of the second harmonic.
questions.instruction: Show derivation from the fundamental mode.

questions.title: Second Harmonic Screenshot
questions.description: Set frequency to second harmonic, restart, wait ~15s, and capture vibration.
questions.instruction: Attach/submit screenshot reference and annotate key features.

questions.title: Nodes and Antinodes (Second Harmonic)
questions.description: Measure positions of nodes and antinodes for second harmonic.
questions.instruction: Provide measured values with units.

questions.title: Harmonic Identification at 0.24 Hz
questions.description: Set frequency to 0.24 Hz, restart, wait ~15s, screenshot vibration, and identify harmonic order.
questions.instruction: Submit screenshot reference and explain how you identified the harmonic.

questions.title: Nodes and Antinodes (0.24 Hz)
questions.description: Measure positions of nodes and antinodes at 0.24 Hz.
questions.instruction: Provide full measured table/list with units.

questions.title: 6th Harmonic Schematic
questions.description: Draw a schematic of the cord vibrating at the 6th harmonic.
questions.instruction: Attach drawing reference and explain marked nodes/antinodes.

questions.title: Calculated Node Positions at 6th Harmonic
questions.description: Determine node positions by calculation at the 6th harmonic.
questions.instruction: Show equations and all computed positions.

questions.title: 6th Harmonic Screenshot
questions.description: Set frequency to 6th harmonic, restart, wait ~15s, and capture vibration.
questions.instruction: Submit screenshot reference and short interpretation.

questions.title: Measured vs Calculated Node/Antinode Positions
questions.description: Measure positions of nodes/antinodes and compare with calculated values.
questions.instruction: Present comparison and discuss discrepancies.

questions.title: Effect of High Tension on Wave Speed
questions.description: Set tension to High and measure wave speed again. Compare with previous value.
questions.instruction: State whether values match and justify physically.

questions.title: Effect of Tension on Resonant Frequencies
questions.description: Determine whether resonant frequencies are the same under Low and High tension.
questions.instruction: Explain your reasoning using observations and formulas.

questions.title: Effect of Damping (Lots)
questions.description: Set damping level to Lots. Determine whether standing waves are still observable.
questions.instruction: Describe observed behavior and explain why.

questions.title: Conclusion
questions.description: Summarize conclusions from the standing waves investigation.
questions.instruction: Provide a concise conclusion covering resonance, harmonics, and damping/tension impacts.

labs.title: Coulomb's Law
labs.description: Investigate Coulomb's law by observing electrostatic attraction/repulsion and the dependence of force on charges and distance.
objectives: Verify proportionality of electrostatic force with product of charges
Estimate Coulomb's constant from experimental slope
Compare measured and theoretical Coulomb constants

metadata.institution: SMU, MedTech, Mediterranean Institute of Technology

questions.title: Table 1: Fixed Distance, Changing Charge
questions.description: Run experiment IV.1 and complete data results table.
questions.instruction: Record all measured values for fixed distance while varying charge.

questions.title: Effect of Doubling $q_2$
questions.description: What happens to $F_{21}$ when $q_2$ is doubled while $q_1$ is constant?
questions.instruction: Select answer and justify with data/theory.

questions.title: Effect of Reducing $q_1$ by Factor 3
questions.description: If $q_2$ is constant and $q_1$ is reduced by factor 3, what happens to $F_{21}$?
questions.instruction: Provide justified conclusion from data.

questions.title: Table 2: Fixed Charge, Changing Distance
questions.description: Run experiment V.1 and complete data results table.
questions.instruction: Record values while varying distance with fixed charges.

questions.title: $F_e$ vs Distance Relationship
questions.description: Describe the relationship between electrostatic force $F_e$ and distance $r$.
questions.instruction: Support answer using Table 2 trends.

questions.title: Graph: $r$ vs $F_{12}$
questions.description: Plot points $(X=r, Y=F_{12})$, add legend, and comment graph.
questions.instruction: Use Table 2 columns 4 and 6 values.

questions.title: Graph: $1/r^2$ vs $F_{12}$
questions.description: Plot points $(X=1/r^2, Y=F_{12})$, add legend, and comment graph.
questions.instruction: Use Table 2 columns 5 and 6 values.

questions.title: Slope and Coulomb Constant
questions.description: Measure the slope of the curve and deduce Coulomb constant $K_c$.
questions.instruction: Show method and final value.

questions.title: $k_{meas}$ vs $k_{theo}$ Comparison
questions.description: Compare experimental Coulomb constant with theoretical value and compute percent error.
questions.instruction: Provide numerical comparison and interpretation.

questions.title: Conclusion
questions.description: What did you learn from studying Coulomb's Law?
questions.instruction: Include: attractive/repulsive behavior, effect of distance, effect of charge, and Newton's third law.

labs.title: Laws of Collisions
labs.description: Explore conservation of momentum and mechanical energy in elastic and inelastic collisions using two carts on a frictionless horizontal track.
objectives: Verify momentum conservation in elastic and inelastic collisions
Compare measured post-collision velocities with theoretical formulas
Analyze graphical relationships between initial and final velocities
Compare measured and predicted collision ratios

questions.title: Elastic Test: $m_1=m_2=1\,kg$, $v_2=0$, $v_1=20$
questions.description: Run elastic test with specified values and fill the table.
questions.instruction: Record pre/post-collision values and calculated quantities.

questions.title: Elastic Test: $m_1 = 2m_2 = 2\,kg$, $v_2 = 0$, $v_1 = 20$
questions.description: Run elastic test with second mass configuration and fill table.
questions.instruction: Record complete dataset and compare with previous run.

questions.title: Elastic Formula Agreement
questions.description: Check whether measured post-collision velocities agree with equation (3).
questions.instruction: Show comparison and explain deviations if any.

questions.title: Head-on Elastic Case
questions.description: For $m_1=m_2=1\,kg$, $v_2=-20\,m/s$, $v_1=20\,m/s$, determine post-collision outcome.
questions.instruction: Provide computed velocities and interpretation.

questions.title: Elastic Collision Deduction
questions.description: State what can be deduced/confirmed from elastic collision experiments.
questions.instruction: Summarize key conservation-law conclusions.

questions.title: Inelastic Test: $m_1=m_2=1\,kg$, $v_2=0$, $v_1=20$
questions.description: Run inelastic test with first configuration and fill table.
questions.instruction: Record measured and computed values.

questions.title: Inelastic Test: $m_1 = 2m_2 = 2\,kg$, $v_2 = 0$, $v_1 = 20$
questions.description: Run inelastic test with second configuration and fill table.
questions.instruction: Record all observations and values.

questions.title: Inelastic Formula Agreement
questions.description: Check whether measured post-collision velocities agree with equation (4).
questions.instruction: Provide quantitative and qualitative comparison.

questions.title: Head-on Inelastic Case
questions.description: For $m_1=m_2=1\,kg$, $v_2=-10\,m/s$, $v_1=20\,m/s$, determine post-collision outcome.
questions.instruction: Show calculations and discuss result.

questions.title: Inelastic Collision Deduction
questions.description: State what can be deduced/confirmed from inelastic collision experiments.
questions.instruction: Focus on momentum and kinetic energy behavior.

questions.title: Velocity Sweep Data Table
questions.description: Repeat for different $v_1$ values and record corresponding $v_2'$.
questions.instruction: Gather complete table of measured values.

questions.title: Plot $v_1$ vs $v_2'$
questions.description: Plot $(X=v_1, Y=v_2')$ points, add legend, and comment the graph.
questions.instruction: Use data from T8 and describe trend.

questions.title: Graphical Slope $C'$
questions.description: Find slope $C'$ of $v_2'$ versus $v_1$ by graphical method.
questions.instruction: Show construction/fit method and computed slope.

questions.title: Direct Ratio C
questions.description: Calculate ratio C directly from measured m1 and m2.
questions.instruction: Provide formula and value.

questions.title: Compare C(mea) and C(pre)
questions.description: Compare measured and predicted ratios.
questions.instruction: Discuss agreement and sources of error.

questions.title: Conclusion
questions.description: Summarize what is learned and discuss whether this is a complete study of collision laws.
questions.instruction: Mention missing cases and explain.
